LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FERGUS MORONEY AND MICHAEL DOOLEY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF FLORIAN GOETTES COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. UNDER THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS, DEFENDANTS HAVE ESTABLISHED THAT THE MALICIOUS PROSECUTION AND CIVIL CONSPIRACY CLAIMS ARISE FROM ACTIVITY PROTECTED BY THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT HE SENT EMAILS THAT CONTAINED VIOLENCE, OBSCENITIES, AND WORDS THAT REFERENCED FUTURE INTERACTION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS. (PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT, P. 5.) PLAINTIFF'S CONDUCT WAS REASONABLY THREATENING AND DEFENDANTS ACCORDINGLY FILED A COMPLAINT WITH SFPD. UNDER THE SECOND PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON HIS MALICIOUS PROSECUTION CLAIM. WHETHER PROBABLE CAUSE EXISTS FOR A CRIMINAL PROSECUTION DEPENDS ON IF IT WAS OBJECTIVELY REASONABLE FOR THE DEFENDANT TO SUSPECT THE PLAINTIFF HAD COMMITTED A CRIME. HERE, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT HE WAS ENGAGED IN AN ONGOING BUSINESS DISPUTE WITH DEFENDANTS, DEFENDANTS KNEW THAT PLAINTIFF HAD JUST LOST HIS JOB AND SHORTLY AFTER SENT AN EMAIL WITH VIOLENCE, PROFANITY AND LANGUAGE THAT A REASONABLE PERSON WOULD FIND THREATENING. (PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT, P. 5.) PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT HIS CLAIM FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION PARALLELS HIS CLAIM FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT APPLIES ITS ANTI-SLAPP ANALYSIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR CIVIL CONSPIRACY. S INOPPORTUNE AS PLAINTIFF'S EXPERIENCES WERE WITH DEFENDANTS, HIS CLAIMS NONETHELESS FALL WITHIN THE PURVIEW OF SECTION 425.15 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS DENIED. THE COURT TO LOOK AT THE PROPOSED ORDERS SUBMITTED BY THE DEFENDANTS REQUESTING FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EHG

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING THE INVOLVEMENT OF JUDGE KAHN AS A WITNESS IN A CASE WHEREIN HE DISMISSED CHARGES AGAINST MR. GOETTE. MR. CLOUGH WAS SWORN IN OPEN COURT REGARDING A CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE AGAINST JUDGE KAHN. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS FERGUS MORONEY AND MICHAEL DOOLEY'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF FLORIAN GOETTES COMPLAINT; REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEE AWARD IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 6, 2015 TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE GOLDSMITH. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SERVES THE COURT WITH A NOTICE AND MOTION TO DISQUALIFY -- C.C.P. SEC. 170.1(A)(6)(III) AS TO JUDGE JOSEPH QUINN. THE COURT MAKES DISCLOSURE RE: PROFESSIONAL RELATIONSHIP WITH ATTORNEY JAMIE DUPREE, AND ENDORSEMENT BY AND MEMBERSHIP WITH BALIF. THE COURT STATES SEEING NO BASIS FOR DISQUALIFICATION. TO ALLOW THE DISQUALIFICATION PROCESS TO BE CARRIED OUT, THE COURT CONTINUES DEFENDANTS FERGUS MORONEY AND MICHAEL DOOLEY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS FROM FEBRUARY 9, 2016 TO MARCH 25, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Defendants Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Florian Goettes Complaint; Request For Attorney Fee Award
Off Calendar Nov-06-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL CLOUGH, TEL. NO. 1-650-271-7746 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAIME G. TOUCHSTONE, TEL. NO. 1-415-399-3906 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS FERGUS MORONEY AND MICHAEL DOOLEY'S Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff Florian Goettes Complaint; Request For Attorney Fee Award - Defendant's Special Motion to Strike is GRANTED. Under the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Defendants have established that the malicious prosecution and civil conspiracy claims arise from activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. "Our Supreme Court has emphasized that the exception for illegal activity is very narrow and applies only in undisputed cases of illegality." (Zucchet v. Galardi (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1466, 1478.) Plaintiff concedes that he sent emails that contained violence, obscenities, and words that referenced future interaction between Plaintiff and Defendants ("we still got unfinished business, you f****** p*****.") (Plaintiff's Complaint, p. 5.) "[T]his is not the rare case in which the illegality of those activities is uncontested or conclusively established." (Zucchet v. Galardi (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 1466, 1480.) Plaintiff's conduct was reasonably threatening and Defendants accordingly filed a complaint with SFPD. Under the second prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Plaintiff has not established a probability of prevailing on his malicious prosecution Claim. Whether probable cause exists for a criminal prosecution depends on if it was objectively reasonable for the defendant to suspect the plaintiff had committed a crime. (Sheldon Appel Co. v. Albert & Oliker (1989) 47 Cal.3d 863, 872.) Here, Plaintiff concedes that he was engaged in an ongoing business dispute with Defendants, Defendants knew that Plaintiff had just lost his job and shortly after sent an email with violence, profanity and language that a reasonable person would find threatening ("we still got unfinished business, you f****** p*****.") (Plaintiff's Complaint, p. 5.) Plaintiff concedes that his claim for malicious prosecution parallels his claim for civil conspiracy. Accordingly, the Court applies its anti-SLAPP analysis for Plaintiff's claim for malicious prosecution to Plaintiff's claim for civil conspiracy. As inopportune as Plaintiff's experiences were with Defendants, his claims nonetheless fall within the purview of section 425.15 of the Code of Civil Procedure. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. Plaintiff's Request for Attorney's Fees is DENIED. The court to look at the Proposed orders submitted by the Defendants requesting for attorney's fees. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
